evidence.claim: TSC1/TSC2 (hamartin-tuberin) is the GAP for Rheb; loss -> constitutive Rheb-GTP -> persistent mTORC1 activation; CNS-specific roles shaping neural connectivity.
mTORC1 drives S6K1/4E-BP1 phosphorylation + GABAergic interneuron dysfunction -> hyperexcitable, structurally abnormal neurons (epileptogenesis).
>2,000 inactivating TSC1/TSC2 variants all cause constitutive mTOR; mTOR inhibitors (rapamycin/everolimus) reduce SEGA + AML burden and seizure frequency.
TSC2 mutations (~75% of cases) associate with MORE SEVERE neurological phenotype than TSC1 (more tubers, higher seizure frequency, more intellectual disability) - 224-patient cohort.
~85% of children/adolescents with TSC develop CNS complications (dominant source of morbidity/mortality).
Cortical tubers arise from biallelic TSC2 loss with mTORC1 molecular footprints; their burden determines seizure/cognitive outcome (Lancet Neurology review).
SEGAs arise via two-hit biallelic TSC1/TSC2 inactivation; long-term mTOR inhibition (everolimus) reduces SEGA volume and prevents hydrocephalus.
Epilepsy affects 75-90% of individuals with TSC, the most common neurological presentation; often refractory despite polypharmacy.
TSC-associated neuropsychiatric disorders (TAND): ASD, intellectual disability, ADHD - core care component per updated international consensus.

notes: Open Targets genetic association TSC2-TSC = 0.951 (one of the highest in the platform); cortical tubers 0.716; ASD 0.566. Everolimus FDA-approved for SEGA (2010) + refractory partial-onset seizures (2018). Citation verification in progress at capture time.
